description_long: This is a pair of pliers featuring elongated, slightly curved handles with a dual-color scheme of red and blue, likely made from a combination of metal and insulated rubber or plastic. The handles are ergonomically shaped for a comfortable grip and appear to have a grippy, rubberized surface for slip resistance. The jaws are flat and squared off, with a unique open-ended design that distinguishes them from standard needle-nose or diagonal pliers, and they do not appear to have serrations or a wire cutter. The pivot mechanism is a fixed, single-axis joint, allowing the jaws to open and close smoothly. The metal components are exposed at the jaws and pivot, while the handles are fully coated. A brand name or logo is visible in red on the blue section of the handle.
description: A pair of ergonomic, red and blue pliers with flat, open-ended jaws and rubberized, curved handles. The tool features a visible brand marking and a fixed pivot joint.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the pliers, highlighting the curved, dual-color handles and the flat, open-ended jaws.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the open jaws and the inner gripping surfaces of the handles, emphasizing the tool's width and jaw geometry.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the other side of the handles and the jaw alignment.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the handles and the open jaws, providing a clear look at the handle curvature and pivot area.
attribute_handle_type: Curved, ergonomic handles with red and blue rubberized insulation for enhanced grip and comfort.
attribute_jaw_design: Flat, squared, open-ended jaws without serrations or wire cutter, designed for gripping or manipulating flat objects.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Fixed, single-axis pivot joint allowing standard opening and closing motion.
attribute_grip_pattern: Rubberized, grippy surface with smooth and contoured ergonomic shaping.
